tip: Download your tickets to your phone AND take a screenshot of the QR code. Cell service can be unreliable near packed stadiums.
Arrive at least 90 minutes before kickoff. The first hour after gates open has the shortest security lines.
Use a clear bag to speed through security. Backpacks and opaque bags are NOT allowed.
Bring an empty reusable water bottle. You can fill it at free water stations inside the stadium.
Wear comfortable walking shoes. You'll walk 2-3 miles on average inside a stadium.
Check the weather forecast and dress in layers. Open-air stadiums can cool down at night.
Visit food courts and restrooms early in each half. Halftime queues can be 15-20 minutes long.
Bring a portable phone charger. You'll need your phone for tickets, maps, and photos.
Learn the nearest emergency exit to your seat BEFORE the match starts.
Use public transit if possible. Parking lots fill up fast and post-match traffic can take 60-90 minutes to clear.
Apply sunscreen before arriving. Even under a retractable roof, UV exposure can be significant.
Take a photo of your parking spot number/location. It's easy to get disoriented after the match.
Set a meeting point with your group in case you get separated. The Information Desk near Gate A is a good default.
Don't bring prohibited items — they'll be confiscated at security and won't be returned.
Cash is accepted but credit/debit cards and mobile payments are faster. Many vendors prefer cashless.
If you have accessibility needs, contact the stadium's accessibility hotline in advance to arrange assistance.
Leave your seat and grab food/drinks during play, not at halftime. The concourse is much quieter during the first and second halves.
The Fan Zone outside the stadium often has free entertainment, food vendors, and activities. Arrive early to enjoy it.
Face paint and small handheld flags are allowed. Large flags on poles and noisemakers like air horns are NOT.
For the best post-match exit, leave 5 minutes early or wait 30 minutes after the final whistle.
Keep your ticket accessible on your phone throughout the event. You may need to show it when moving between levels.
If it starts raining at an open-air stadium, concourse areas provide shelter. Pack a poncho just in case.
Binoculars are allowed and highly recommended for upper-level seats.
Non-aerosol sunscreen under 100ml is allowed through security.
Baby formula, food, and diapers are allowed through security. Nursing rooms are available at every stadium.
